root_text.an9.25:1.1: “Yato kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno kallaṁ vacanāya:
root_text.an9.25:1.2: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an9.25:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti?
root_text.an9.25:2.2: ‘Vītarāgaṁ me cittan’ti paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.25:2.3: ‘vītadosaṁ me cittan’ti paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.25:2.4: ‘vītamohaṁ me cittan’ti paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.25:2.5: ‘asarāgadhammaṁ me cittan’ti paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.25:2.6: ‘asadosadhammaṁ me cittan’ti paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.25:2.7: ‘asamohadhammaṁ me cittan’ti paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.25:2.8: ‘anāvattidhammaṁ me cittaṁ kāmabhavāyā’ti paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.25:2.9: ‘anāvattidhammaṁ me cittaṁ rūpabhavāyā’ti paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti;
root_text.an9.25:2.10: ‘anāvattidhammaṁ me cittaṁ arūpabhavāyā’ti paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti.
root_text.an9.25:2.11: Yato kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno paññāya cittaṁ suparicitaṁ hoti, tassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno kallaṁ vacanāya:
root_text.an9.25:2.12: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’”ti.

translation_text.an9.25:1.1: “Mendicants, when a mendicant’s mind has been well consolidated with wisdom it’s appropriate for them to say:
translation_text.an9.25:1.2: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.”’
translation_text.an9.25:2.1: And how is a mendicant’s mind well consolidated with wisdom?
translation_text.an9.25:2.2: The mind is well consolidated with wisdom when they know: ‘My mind is without greed.’
translation_text.an9.25:2.5: … ‘My mind is not liable to become greedy.’
translation_text.an9.25:2.6: … ‘My mind is not liable to become hateful.’
translation_text.an9.25:2.7: … ‘My mind is not liable to become deluded.’
translation_text.an9.25:2.8: … ‘My mind is not liable to return to rebirth in the sensual realm.’
translation_text.an9.25:2.9: … ‘My mind is not liable to return to rebirth in the realm of luminous form.’
translation_text.an9.25:2.10: … ‘My mind is not liable to return to rebirth in the formless realm.’
translation_text.an9.25:2.11: When a mendicant’s mind has been well consolidated with wisdom it’s appropriate for them to say:
translation_text.an9.25:2.12: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.”’”
